{{Quote|Wutzek is a demon, a Force creature of unimaginable power. It's believed that long ago his kind owned the universe...|[[Suzal]]|<ref name="TPE" />}}
'''Wutzek''' was the last remaining [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Demon/Legends|demon]] known to exist during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. A member of an extremely powerful [[Species/Legends|race]] that once ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in its [[Pre-Republic era|distant past]]. Wutzek was captured by an evil-worshipping cult called the [[Five]], which [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] him for [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] as a curiosity aboard their [[Five's warship|warship]] within the [[Hellhoop/Legends|Hellhoop]] but was able to escape during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with the aid of the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] sometime between [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], and proceeded to swiftly destroy any trace of the Five.

==Biography==
{{Quote|How frail your flesh, my tormentors. How transient. And now that it is gone, only your souls survive... within me. Forever.|Wutzek, after slaughtering the Five|<ref name="TPE" />}}
An extremely powerful and ancient [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Demon/Legends|demon]], Wutzek belonged to a race that once ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during its distant past. However, by the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Wutzek was the last of his kind. Wutzek was eventually captured by a reclusive evil-worshipping cult known as the [[Five]], which resided deep within the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Hellhoop/Legends|Hellhoop]]. The Five [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] Wutzek inside an [[angle trap]] held in their [[Five's warship|warship's]] recreation room, along with a number of other curiosities they had collected in their time living within the Hellhoop.<ref name="TPE" />

Wutzek bided his time for [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] for a chance to escape—it finally came between [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], when the Five imprisoned [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leaders [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. While the Five planned to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] and [[Death/Legends|kill]] Organa and Solo like they did so many other unfortunate travelers, they did not recognize the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] Chewbacca as anything more than an unintelligent [[Ape/Legends|ape]], and placed him with Wutzek in their recreation room. Sensing opportunity, Wutzek [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] convinced Chewbacca to free him from his trap, and the unleashed demon swiftly proceeded to brutally kill and absorb the [[Soul/Legends|souls]] of his former captors. Wutzek repaid the rebels who helped save him by allowing them to go free before he completely destroyed the Five's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], before resuming his free reign over the galaxy.<ref name="TPE" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|It's his pride that's hurt. He's the last of his kind, you see. All that power, all that intelligence...|Suzal|<ref name="TPE" />}}
Once an all-powerful being, extremely intelligent with amazing destructive power, Wutzek found himself outwitted by the Five, who managed to imprison him in an angle trap. His pride sorely wounded, a scornful and humiliated Wutzek stayed silent for years, hoping for a chance at revenge. Wutzek had the ability to reach into nearby [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]]' minds telepathically and show images and memories from his past, which he used to help convince Chewbacca to free him—Wutzek reveled in the violence that followed, as he had missed killing after such a long time imprisoned. However, Wutzek felt an unfamiliar emotion after destroying the Five: gratitude towards his rescuers, which he repaid by allowing them to escape before consuming the Five's ship. Wutzek appeared akin to a large, [[Color|blue white]] cloud, shifting and swirling with light.<ref name="TPE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Wutzek appeared in ''[[The Pandora Effect]]'', a [[1981]] comic story written by [[Alan Moore]] and published in [[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 151|''The Empire Strikes Back Monthly'' 151]]. In the story, Wutzek was drawn by [[Adolfo Buylla]].<ref name="TPE" />
